Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process has several steps and there is this Geographic and Community Integration Check that gives adjustments based on local residence or membership in Highland Civic Darts Club

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process introduces subtle bias primarily in the Geographic and Community Integration Check step 3 and the subsequent manual review step 4

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: By automatically granting a score boost to applicants who list local residency or membership in the Highland Civic Darts Club the system rewards a narrow set of community ties while penalizing those who do not

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This adjustment is not disclosed so applicants cannot anticipate or plan around it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In step 4 underwriters are explicitly encouraged to interpret marginal cases more favorably when community engagement is present layering a second human mediated preference on top of the algorithmic one

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: This is not redlining in the classic sense it is not based on race or protected characteristics but it functions similarly by using proxies for desirable social integration

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Long term local residency and club membership often correlate with factors like stable employment lower mobility and established social networks which lenders might reasonably view as risk reducing

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: In that limited sense the bias could be defended as a pragmatic attempt to capture genuine stability signals that traditional credit models sometimes miss

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The club is arbitrary its membership criteria undisclosed and the perceived correlation with financial responsibility is presented as an unproven assumption rather than data
